name: Nicaragua

colors.symbolism: the two oceans bordering Nicaragua and the country's commitment to justice, loyalty and truth
the territory between the two oceans, peace, integrity and the purity of the nation
the sun and golden rays within the coat of arms, and the equality of all citizens
the lush land and the Phrygian cap of liberty within the coat of arms
the Phrygian cap of liberty within the coat of arms, signifying freedom

description: Three equal horizontal stripes — blue, white, blue — with the national coat of arms centred on the white band: an equilateral triangle containing five volcanoes between two seas, a Phrygian cap of liberty radiating golden rays, and a rainbow, surrounded by the inscription 'REPUBLICA DE NICARAGUA / AMERICA CENTRAL'.
history: The blue-white-blue tricolour with a central emblem derives directly from the flag of the Federal Republic of Central America (1823–1839), of which Nicaragua was a member state. After several variants in the 19th century, the current design was officially re-established by law on 4 September 1908 and confirmed in subsequent legislation; the precise shades and coat-of-arms detail were standardised by Decree No. 1908 of 27 August 1971.

features.symbolism: the two blue stripes represent the Pacific Ocean and the Caribbean Sea bordering the country, while the white centre stripe represents the land between them and the purity of the nation

a triangle (equality, justice) encloses five volcanoes representing the five original member-states of the Federal Republic of Central America, set on the isthmus between the Pacific Ocean and Caribbean Sea, surmounted by a Phrygian cap of liberty radiating golden rays and a rainbow of peace; the inscription 'REPUBLICA DE NICARAGUA' encircles the upper border and 'AMERICA CENTRAL' the lower
